signals.headline: Two-Week Iran Ceasefire Holding; U
signals.raw:
Two-Week Iran Ceasefire Holding; U.S. and Iran Considering Extended Negotiations** | YES | HIGH confidence

A **two-week ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is currently in effect and set to expire on April 21st**[2][3]. The ceasefire was announced by President Trump ahead of an 8 p.m. deadline on April 7th, contingent on Iran opening the Strait of Hormuz[1].

signals.headline: **Market reaction positive**: Stocks rose and oil declined as these ceasefire extension talks were announced, indicating
signals.raw: **Market reaction positive**: Stocks rose and oil declined as these ceasefire extension talks were announced, indicating investor confidence in a longer-term deal[3].

signals.headline: **Nuclear negotiations narrowing**: The U
signals.raw: **Nuclear negotiations narrowing**: The U.S. and Iran have traded proposals on Iranian nuclear activities, with Iran proposing a five-year suspension versus the U.S.'s initial demand for a 20-year moratorium[4]. This represents movement toward potential compromise.

signals.headline: **Countervailing pressure**: Trump simultaneously initiated a U
signals.raw:
**Countervailing pressure**: Trump simultaneously initiated a U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz to increase pressure on Tehran[3], though China has warned against obstruction of Chinese vessels[4].

The immediate market-moving confirmation is that substantive negotiations are resuming before the April 21st ceasefire expiration, reducing the risk of immediate escalation.

signals.headline: SOUTH KOREA MARCH 2026 ANNUAL INFLATION RISES TO 2
signals.raw:
SOUTH KOREA MARCH 2026 ANNUAL INFLATION RISES TO 2.2% | NO | HIGH confidence**  
South Korea's annual inflation rate increased to 2.2% in March 2026 from 2.0% in January and February, below market forecasts of 2.4%.[1] This figure aligns closely with another report of 2.16% year-over-year for March, up from 2.00% prior.[3]  
No evidence of announcements or resolutions today (April 14, 2026) impact

signals.headline: **Active negotiations resuming**: The U
signals.raw: **Active negotiations resuming**: The U.S. and Iran are weighing a return to peace talks in Pakistan to extend the ceasefire before it expires[3]. Reuters and the Associated Press report negotiations could resume as early as the end of this week, though no date has been finalized[4].
